title: sarcophrynium prionogonium var. ivorense
intro: Sarcophrynium prionogonium var. ivorense is a variety of the Sarcophrynium prionogonium plant species, commonly known as the 'Yellow Flag' or 'Yellow Sedge', which belongs to the family Marantaceae. It is native to West Africa, particularly in the tropical regions of the Ivory Coast. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, and is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use by making a decoction of its leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
